title: wormia bracteata
intro: Wormia bracteata, also known as the "Dwarf Wormia" or "Wild Wormia", is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is primarily found in the tropical forests of India, where its roots and leaves have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Wormia bracteata are attributed to its ability to treat various ailments, such as fever, digestive issues, and skin problems, while also exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Traditional herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures of the plant's roots and leaves, which are often used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
